title: rivea zeylanica
intro: Rivea zeylanica, also known as Moonseed, is a plant species native to tropical Africa and Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine. The plant contains a toxic compound called taxine, but when used in moderation and under proper guidance, it has been shown to possess health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving digestion. Rivea zeylanica can be prepared into various herbal preparations including decoctions, infusions, and powder, often used to treat conditions like fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
